headline: Mauritius FIRST BASELINE (rotation substitute) — Published 60.0 Functional confirmed. Stable parliamentary democracy; strong economic governance (upper-middle-income); 2024 ICJ ruling on Chagos sovereignty resolved long-standing structural-historical claim. UK transfer of Chagos sovereignty negotiated (October 2024). Concerns: media independence pressures; opposition complaints about institutional capture; 2024 election irregularity claims. Decriminalization of same-sex relations (2023 Supreme Court). Net assessment: confirm at 60.0 Functional, with internal +1.5 dimensional pressure from Chagos resolution (SYS + INT carry-forward); sub-threshold.

scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.4 unchanged. Chagos resolution is a S4 Structural Critique positive but historical context still being processed.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.3 unchanged. Chagos sovereignty pursuit was multi-generation consistency-under-pressure positive.

key_evidence: Stable parliamentary democracy; upper-middle-income economic governance.
2024 ICJ ruling on Chagos sovereignty; UK transfer of Chagos sovereignty negotiated October 2024.
2023 Supreme Court decriminalization of same-sex relations.
Concerns: media independence pressures; opposition complaints about institutional capture; 2024 election irregularity claims.

evidence.finding: 60.0 Functional placement reflects stable democratic governance and Chagos resolution.

math_check.composite_calculation: ((3.3375 - 1) / 4) * 100 = 58.4 (canonical published 60.0 reflects slight scoring rounding by original assessor)
math_check.rounding_note: Canonical published value used (60.0). Math-hygiene gap of +1.6 noted but not flagged as new cluster entry (below cluster threshold).
math_check.validator_check: First baseline confirmation at canonical 60.0 Functional.

negativeSignals: Media independence pressures; opposition institutional-capture complaints; 2024 election irregularity claims.
positiveSignals: Chagos sovereignty resolution; 2023 same-sex decriminalization; stable parliamentary democracy.
